metric_value: Single-sideband FM enabling real-time bidirectional broadcast quality NTSC PAL S-VHS video and stereo audio over twisted pair
notes: Aberdeen describes demo as like broadcast TV quality. Two-way real-time. No rewiring — uses existing phone line.

metric_value: Broadcast TV quality maintained across 4000 feet of twisted pair phone line
notes: Aberdeen: even across 4000 feet quality of viewer experience is like traditional TV.

metric_value: Below $3000 per user in useful configuration
notes: Switch (5-100 users) installed next to PBX + modem-sized desktop device. Available summer 1997.

metric_value: Switch handles 5 to 100 users; interfaces with ATM ISDN coax DSS
notes: No rewiring required. Interfaces with most WAN transports.

metric_value: IS shops with high-bandwidth WAN infrastructure should trial VidModem to fast-track multimedia learning curve
notes: Aberdeen recommendation for IS managers who have widened WAN pipes and are evaluating last-thousand-feet multimedia solutions.

metric_value: Objective Communications did not survive as independent company; VidModem technology displaced by IP video
notes: No evidence of Objective Communications surviving beyond late 1990s. IP-based H.323 and later SIP/WebRTC displaced the analog FM approach entirely.

metric_value: Telephone carriers lined up behind VidModem for ATM promotion but dependency on telco buildout poses risk (like ISDN)
notes: Aberdeen explicitly warns: many an application has died on the vine waiting for telcos to build out their own infrastructures (remember ISDN).

metric_value: VidModem and ATM service both declined; IP-based broadband and internet video bypassed the telco ATM path entirely
notes: Aberdeen's ISDN warning proved prescient. ATM declined as IP/Ethernet prevailed. Broadband internet (DSL cable) enabled IP video without telco ATM dependency.

metric_value: Best suited for call centers banking/finance ATM kiosks medical/imaging manufacturing shop floor military/field training
notes: Aberdeen identifies specific use cases where broadcast video quality justifies VidModem deployment.

metric_value: H.323 SIP and IP video conferencing displaced analog approaches; Cisco Polycom Tandberg dominated enterprise video
notes: IP-based video conferencing became enterprise standard. WebRTC and cloud video (Zoom Teams) completed the transition by 2020.

metric_value: Overcomes bandwidth CPU drain installation complexity and cost barriers of existing computer-analog integration schemes
notes: Aberdeen identifies four specific barriers VidModem overcomes vs digital multimedia approaches.

metric_value: VidModem enables CTVI — Computer Telephony Video Integration — seamless segue from phone call to videoconference to document sharing
notes: Aberdeen coins CTVI term. The concept proved durable — modern UC platforms (Teams Zoom) deliver exactly this integration via IP.

metric_value: VidModem scales more easily than current multimedia-to-desktop solutions as IS upgrades WAN or incorporates different appliances
notes: Aberdeen cites scalability and economic upgrade path as competitive advantages.

metric_value: Objective should cultivate partnerships beyond telcos — ISPs NC players PC manufacturers cable companies
notes: Aberdeen explicitly advises Objective to diversify beyond telco ATM partners to ISPs NC vendors PC makers cable companies.

metric_value: Zoom Microsoft Teams Google Meet WebRTC-based platforms fulfilled enterprise multimedia vision Aberdeen described
notes: Aberdeen's vision of ubiquitous enterprise multimedia fully realized by 2020 cloud video platforms — via IP broadband not phone-line FM.

metric_value: Existing twisted pair phone wire viable as multimedia transport with VidModem — no rewiring required
notes: Aberdeen: phone wire is cheap it's there and with VidModem it's a viable multimedia transport.

metric_value: DSL used same twisted pair for broadband internet enabling IP video — different solution same physical medium insight
notes: Aberdeen's insight that existing phone wire is sufficient multimedia transport proved correct — DSL used twisted pair for broadband; VidModem's analog FM approach was superseded.
